scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: 7 read streams (contacts, deals/opportunity, tasks, milestone/pipelines, campaigns/workflows, tickets_filters, and a fan_out-driven tickets stream sourced from tickets_filters) and 9 write actions covering contact/deal/task create, partial-update, and delete. Paths below are relative to `/dev/api` (this bundle's `base_url` already includes that prefix; `streams.json`'s stream paths start at the resource segment). AgileCRM models companies as `type: COMPANY` rows in the SAME `/contacts` collection as person contacts, so create_contact/update_contact/delete_contact already cover company create/update/delete too (not separately listed below). Excluded: the dedicated companies-list endpoint (POST-only with form-encoded paging params; this dialect's stream reads are GET-only, and company rows remain reachable via the contacts stream regardless); events (the list endpoint requires a caller-supplied start/end epoch time window with no documented default or discovery endpoint, so there is no fixed/config-defaultable value this dialect can declare that is globally correct for an arbitrary sync); notes/documents (each is a sub-resource enumerable only per-contact/per-deal, i.e. would need a second fan_out hop layered on an already-covered parent stream, deferred this pass); email/phone/dynamic-filter contact lookups (single-record lookups keyed by a caller-supplied value, not list-all streams); and narrow single-field/bulk/notification-side-effect endpoints (tag/score/star/owner mutation via dedicated non-standard paths, bulk delete, campaign enrollment, ticket creation/notes) which are narrower slices of the general create/update/delete actions already modeled.

excluded.reason: single-contact detail; identical record shape to the contacts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-field (lead_score) partial mutation; update_contact's edit-properties call already covers general contact field updates
single-field (star_value) partial mutation; narrower than update_contact's general field-update coverage
tag-add sub-mutation distinct from general property update; narrow scope deferred this pass
tag-remove sub-mutation distinct from general property update; narrow scope deferred this pass
single-record lookup keyed by a caller-supplied email, not a list-all/syncable stream
alternate request-shape for the same single-contact-by-email lookup as the GET variant
free-text contact/company search keyed by a caller-supplied query string, not a list-all/syncable stream
tag-add-by-email sub-mutation; narrower than update_contact's general field-update coverage
tag-remove-by-email sub-mutation; narrower than update_contact's general field-update coverage
single-field (lead_score) increment-by-email mutation; narrower than update_contact's general field-update coverage
single-property-add-by-email mutation; narrower than update_contact's general field-update coverage
owner-reassignment sub-mutation distinct from general property update; narrow scope deferred this pass
enrolls a contact into a marketing campaign by email; a workflow side-effect action, not a record create/update/delete
removes a contact from an active campaign; a workflow side-effect action, not a record create/update/delete
single-record lookup keyed by a caller-supplied phone number, not a list-all/syncable stream
ad hoc dynamic contact filter keyed by caller-supplied tag/custom-field criteria at request time, not a fixed list-all stream

the dedicated companies-list endpoint is POST-only with form-encoded page_size/cursor/global_sort_key params; this dialect's declarative stream reads are GET-only. Company records remain reachable via the contacts stream (AgileCRM models companies as `type: COMPANY` rows in the same contacts collection)
single-deal detail; identical record shape to the deals list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-record lookup keyed by a caller-supplied contact email, not a list-all/syncable stream
bulk multi-id delete; single-record delete_deal already covers the per-record mutation shape this dialect can express
deals grouped by milestone; the same underlying deal records the deals stream already reads, reshaped into a grouping envelope rather than a flat list
deals for one pipeline grouped by milestone; a filtered/regrouped view of the deals stream's own records
deals filtered to one pipeline_id; a filtered subset of the deals stream's own records, not a distinct resource shape
deals related to one already-covered contact; a filtered subset of the deals stream's own records
deals owned by the authenticated API user; a filtered subset of the deals stream's own records
removes one contact association from a deal; a narrow join-table mutation, not a full-record update
single-task detail; identical record shape to the tasks list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
creates a task tied to a contact by email; the same task-creation shape as create_task with an alternate contact-association mechanism
tasks due within N days; a filtered subset of the tasks stream's own records, not a distinct resource shape
tasks filtered by pending/criteria/type; a filtered subset of the tasks stream's own records
tasks related to one already-covered contact, sorted; a filtered subset of the tasks stream's own records

calendar events require caller-supplied start/end epoch query parameters with no documented default or discovery endpoint to derive a globally-correct sync window from; not declarable as a fixed list-all stream
events related to one already-covered contact; the same out-of-scope events resource, additionally filtered
creates a calendar event; part of the out-of-scope events resource (no corresponding read stream to pair a write action with)
updates a calendar event; part of the out-of-scope events resource
deletes a calendar event; part of the out-of-scope events resource
creates a new deal pipeline/milestone track; account-structure configuration rather than a transactional business-object write, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
updates a deal pipeline/milestone track; account-structure configuration, same exclusion as create
irreversibly deletes a pipeline/milestone track structure (not a business record); no allow-listed reverse-ETL delete for account-structure configuration
creates a note tied to one or more contacts; a sub-resource of already-covered parent records that would require a second fan_out hop not modeled this pass
creates a note by contact email; the same out-of-scope notes sub-resource, alternate association mechanism
notes are enumerable only per already-covered parent contact/deal, requiring a second fan_out hop (contacts -> per-contact notes) not modeled this pass; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
deletes a note nested under an already-covered contact; part of the out-of-scope notes sub-resource
creates a note tied to a deal; part of the out-of-scope notes sub-resource
updates a deal note; part of the out-of-scope notes sub-resource
notes nested under an already-covered deal; part of the out-of-scope notes sub-resource, would require a second fan_out hop
bulk multi-contact note creation; part of the out-of-scope notes sub-resource
documents are enumerable only per already-covered parent contact, requiring a second fan_out hop not modeled this pass; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
creates a document tied to a contact; part of the out-of-scope documents sub-resource
updates a document tied to a contact; part of the out-of-scope documents sub-resource
ticket creation is a help-desk workflow object outside this connector's core CRM (contact/deal/task) write scope this pass; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
ticket deletion; same help-desk-workflow-scope exclusion as ticket creation
ticket message thread nested under an already-covered ticket, requiring a second fan_out hop not modeled this pass
adds a message/note to a ticket thread; same help-desk-workflow-scope exclusion
